oatmeal-apple-bread-lezzet.name: Oatmeal Apple Bread
instructions: 1. Take the Yulaf to a large bowl.
2. Add boiling water on it and mix.
3. Wrap over with stretch film and wait for 8-10 minutes for swelling.
4. Blend salt, flour and yeast in a separate mixing container.
5. Add oats and grated apples and half water cups of the bottle and knead it to achieve a soft consistency dough.
6. Wrap over with stretch film and rest for 2 hours.
7. Yeast dough is kneaded on lightly unified countertop.
8. Roll shape with your hands.
9. Take the lighted oven tray and wait for 1 hour for yeast.
10. Cook for about 30 minutes until the yeast dough was previously heated in 200 degrees oven.
11. 11. slice and serve after cooling.

oatmeal-pie.name: Oatmeal Pie

ingredients.item: Recipe pastry for a 9 inch single crust pie

instructions: Mix together eggs, corn syrup, brown sugar, coconut, oats, butter or margarine, and salt. Pour into the unbaked pie shell.
Bake at 163°C (165 degrees C) for 45 minutes, or until golden in color and knife inserted in center comes out clean.

oatmeal-raisin-energy-balls.name: Oatmeal Raisin Energy Balls
instructions: Place almond flour and dates in a food processor and pulse until sticky.
Add the raisins, almond butter, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and salt, and pulse until a sticky dough forms.  Add 1 tablespoon of water if batter seems too dry.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the rolled oats.
Roll the mixture into tablespoon-sized balls and let sit at room temperature for 2-4 hours.
Store in an airtight container for up to 1 week.
Enjoy!

obituary-cocktail.name: Obituary Cocktail
instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the lemon twist in a mixing glass filled with ice and stir until chilled, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Express a lemon twist over the drink and drop into the glass.

octopus-with-juicy-broth-argiro.name: Octopus With Juicy Broth
instructions: Place the octopus in a small pot just enough to fit it.
Add the wine, vinegar, pepper, onion and let it boil.
Cover and boat in modernity until it softens around 40’.
After boiling, strain its broth and fill in water so that we have 1 litre of liquids.
Cut the octopus into thick slices

ode-to-halo-halo.name: Ode To Halo-halo
instructions: Combine bananas, brown sugar, vanilla, a pinch of salt, and 2 Tbsp. water in a small bowl. Let sit, stirring occasionally, until bananas are jammy and liquid is slightly thickened, 20–30 minutes.

Meanwhile, combine berries, lime juice, granulated sugar, and a pinch of salt in a medium bowl. Mash berries lightly to release juices. Let sit, stirring occasionally, until juices are slightly thickened, 15–20 minutes.

Stir coconut milk and sweetened condensed milk in a small bowl to combine. Assemble halo-halo in 2 small bowls or pint glasses, alternating layers of bananas, berries, shaved ice, ice cream, and coconut milk mixture. Top as desired.

old-fashioned-christmas-turkey-teleculin.name: Old-fashioned Christmas Turkey
old-fashioned-christmas-turkey-teleculin.original_name: Peru de Natal à moda antiga

ingredients.item: 100 g pine nuts or palm kernels

instructions: Fix the turkey and put it in a alguidar with seasoned water of thick salt, brandy, oranges and lemons cut into slices. Let it marinate for 24 hours.

On the day, prepare the filling: Peel the onions and garlic and pick them finely. Remove the rind from bacon and ham and pinch them roughly. In a frying pan, heat the olive oil, add the onion and garlic and let it saute for 2 minutes. Add the bacon and ham, stir and let it cook until the onion begins to get golden. Remove from the fire, add the crushed bread core, wrap well, add the yolks, pine nuts and olives, season with salt and pepper and wrap.

Fill the turkey with the previous mixture and fasten the paws with kitchen wire. Place it on an oven tray, water with the olive oil and bar with the lard. Lay some of the marinade on the board, cover with aluminium foil and bring it to the oven preheated at 180oC for 50 minutes.

Peel the pineapple, cut them into small pieces, add them to the tray, add the chestnuts, remove the foil and let it roast another 30 minutes, watering with the sauce itself. Check the baking, remove from the oven and serve decorated to taste.

one-pan-bbq-baby-back-ribs.name: One-pan Bbq Baby Back Ribs
instructions: Preheat oven to 275˚F (140˚C).
Season the ribs with salt, black pepper, paprika, cayenne pepper, oregano, and brown sugar. Rub in the seasonings evenly on both sides.
Place the ribs on a baking sheet lined with foil. Cover the ribs with foil.
Bake for 2 hours.
Increase the oven temperature to 500˚F (260˚C).
Carefully uncover the ribs. Spread BBQ sauce evenly on top of the ribs.
Place green beans on the sides of the pan. Season the green beans with salt.
Bake for 10 minutes.
Transfer the ribs to a cutting board.
Cut between the bones to separate into individual ribs. Serve with green beans.
Enjoy!

one-pan-pork-tenderloin-dinner-with-appl.name: One-pan Pork Tenderloin Dinner With Apples And Onion
instructions: Pat the pork tenderloin dry and generously season with salt and pepper on all sides.
Heat 2 tablespoons of canola oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat, until the oil is shimmering and just starting to smoke.
Place pork in the hot skillet and sear until well browned, about 1½-2 minutes. Flip and repeat searing on all sides.
Remove the pork from the skillet and set aside.
Preheat the oven to 375˚F (190˚C).
Heat the remaining tablespoon of oil in the skillet.
Add the onion to hot skillet and toss to coat in the oil. Add the garlic and apples and cook until the apples and onion begin to soften.
Pour the cider over apples and onion and bring to a simmer.
Return the pork to the skillet, on top of the apple and onion mixture. Spoon the cooking liquid over the pork and transfer the entire skillet to the oven.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, until the internal temperature of the pork reaches 145˚F (62˚C.)
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing. Serve the sliced pork over a bed of the apples and onions.
Enjoy!

onion-naan.name: Onion Naan

ingredients.item: All-purpose flour plus more for surface and hands
Melted ghee (clarified butter) or vegetable oil plus more

instructions: Heat milk in a small saucepan over medium-low heat until an instant-read thermometer registers 100°. Transfer to a small bowl and whisk in yeast and sugar. Let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
Whisk 3½ cups flour and 1 teaspoon salt in a large bowl to blend. Add yeast mixture, onion, yogurt, and 2 tablespoons ghee. Mix dough until blended but still shaggy.

Transfer dough to a lightly floured work surface. Knead until a smooth dough forms, adding flour as needed (dough will be sticky), about 5 minutes. Lightly grease another large bowl with ghee, place dough in bowl, and turn to coat. Cover with plastic wrap. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Punch down dough and divide into 10 pieces. Using floured hands, roll each piece into a ball on a lightly floured surface. Cover with plastic wrap; let rest 10 minutes.

Heat a large cast-iron or other heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Lightly coat with ghee. Working with 1 piece at a time, stretch dough with your hands or roll out with a rolling pin to ⅛ inch thickness. Sprinkle with salt. Cook until lightly blistered, puffed, and cooked through, about 2 minutes per side. Wrap in foil to keep warm until ready to serve.
Do Ahead: Naan dough can be made 4 hours before shaping. Cover and chill.

onion-stuffed-with-bonito-recipe-comeder.name: Onion Stuffed With Bonito: Recipe
onion-stuffed-with-bonito-recipe-comeder.original_name: Cebolla rellena de bonito: receta fácil

ingredients.item: 50 gr de aceitunas negras (sin hueso y picadas)

instructions: Peel the onions and cut them a cover at the top. With a small spoon, empty the centre of each onion, leaving about 1 cm thick on the walls. Keep the inside of the onions you have drawn.
Put water with salt and cook the onions empty for about 10 minutes. Once tender, carefully remove them and let them run onto absorbing paper.

In a frying pan, heat up an olive oil chorrit and add minced garlic. Fry till it's golden. Then add the red pepper, minced onion remains and tomatoes. Cooking at medium heat till everything's well tiled, about 10 minutes. Add the sweet pimenton and remove well.
Drain the crockery and add to the vegetable mix. Also add minced olives. Cooking with fire under about 5 more minutes so that flavors integrate well. Jumped to taste.
It purifies the oven at 180 and 3. Fill every onion with a blend of good and vegetable and put them into an oven plate. Rocía with some olive oil.
Wet the stuffed onions for 20 minutes, or until they are well golden at the top and tender at the inside.
Take out the oven and decorate with fresh parsley minced. Can be served with a plate of fresh salad or mashed potatoes.

orange-dessert-with-honey-and-mint-refre.name: Orange Dessert With Honey And Mint: Refreshing And
orange-dessert-with-honey-and-mint-refre.original_name: Postre de naranjas a la miel y menta: refrescante y fácil

ingredients.item: Hojas de menta fresca al gusto

instructions: Pela las naranjas, eliminando toda la piel blanca para evitar el amargor. Corta las naranjas en rodajas finas.
Put the cuts of orange into a frying pan with medium heat. Add the honey onto the oranges and add 20 ml of rum to the frying pan.
Cocina las naranjas ligeramente, durante unos 3-5 minutos.
Cooking till the oranges start to soften and the honey mingle with the juice of the oranges and form an syrup.
Remove the oranges from the frying pan and place them in a serving plate. Roast the cooking juice onto the oranges. Decor with minced fresh mint leaves and some whole to decorate. Enjoy!

orange-gingerbread-cheesecake-bars.name: Orange-gingerbread Cheesecake Bars
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line a 9x13-inch baking pan with enough parchment paper to have overhang on all sides. Lightly grease parchment.
Whisk together flour, ginger, cinnamon, baking soda, cloves, nutmeg, allspice, and salt for the gingerbread in a large bowl until combined.
Cream brown sugar and butter in a separate bowl with an electric mixed until light and fluffy. Add egg and vanilla extract and beat until thoroughly combined. Mix in molasses.

Add flour mixture to butter mixture in 3 separate additions, mixing only until just combined; dough will be fairly thick. Press 2/3 of the dough firmly and evenly into the bottom of the prepared baking pan, using floured hands to prevent sticking.

Beat cream cheese, sugar, and salt for the filling in a large bowl until completely smooth and fluffy. Add eggs, 1 at a time, mixing well after each addition. Mix in vanilla extract and orange zest. Pour filling evenly over gingerbread crust and smooth with a spatula. Sprinkle remaining gingerbread dough over the filling and gently press down.

Bake in the preheated oven until edges are set and the center of the cheesecake only slightly jiggles, 35 to 40 minutes. Cool completely in pan. Once cooled, remove from pan using parchment 'handles' and slice into bars.

oranges-with-chocolate-teleculinaria.name: Oranges With Chocolate
instructions: Peel the oranges and separate them in buds. Break the chocolate into pieces, melt them in a sea bath, stirring from time to time, and remove from the fire as soon as it is completely melted.

Dry the orange buds well with absorbent paper, dip one end into the melted chocolate and shake the excess. Pass some by grated coconut and others by the chopped seeds, let it dry and serve them cold or at room temperature.

orecchiette-pasta-with-margherita-genoa-.name: Orecchiette Pasta With Margherita® Genoa Salami

ingredients.prep: roasted, skinned, cored, and cut into strips

ingredients.item: Fresh ground black pepper to taste

instructions: In a small pot place 1/4 cup whole peeled garlic cloves. Cover with extra virgin olive oil and cook on low heat until garlic becomes soft and cooked through. Set aside to cool, retain olive oil.

Bring a pot of lightly salted water to boil and cook orecchiette pasta until al dente. Reserve approximately 1/4 cup of the pasta water to add to the end of step 3. Drain pasta and cool in cold water, then set aside.

In a large saute pan, heat garlic oil from roasted garlic. Add Margherita® Genoa salami and brown slightly. Add peppers, spinach, roasted garlic, and half of the toasted pine nuts and continue to cook until spinach is wilted. Add cooked pasta and reserved pasta water, fresh basil and toss, continue to saute until pasta is hot and ready to serve.
Place pasta on plates or serving bowl, sprinkle with grated Romano cheese, black pepper and top with remaining pine nuts.
